value.china_hong_kong_basis: Hong Kong and mainland China reopened after the Friday 2026-06-19 Tuen Ng/Dragon Boat market holiday; their daily moves compare with Thursday 2026-06-18
value.japan_korea_basis: Japan and South Korea used normal Monday local sessions, measured against Friday 2026-06-19 closes

text: Monday, June 22, 2026 was a regular trading day for the referenced Asia-Pacific cash-equity markets. Hong Kong and mainland China reopened after the Friday, June 19 Tuen Ng/Dragon Boat holiday, so Hong Kong and Shanghai daily moves are measured against June 18, while Japan and South Korea are measured against their normal June 19 closes.

nikkei_225.basis: cash close; AP rounds to +1.6%, MarketWatch reports +1.5%

kospi.basis: Yonhap close bulletin; AP rounds to +0.7%

shanghai_composite.basis: WSJ historical table; AP rounds to +1.8%

calculation.method: Nikkei: 72,353.96 minus 1,103.90 implies a prior close near 71,250.06 and +1.55%; KOSPI: 9,114.55 minus 62.13 equals 9,052.42 and +0.69%; Hang Seng: 23,768.52 minus 23,924.81 equals -156.29 and -0.65%; Shanghai: 4,163.10 minus 4,090.48 equals +72.62 and +1.78%

text: Asia-Pacific equity markets closed mixed on Monday, June 22, 2026. The Nikkei 225 rose 1,103.90 points (+1.55%) to 72,353.96, the KOSPI rose 62.13 points (+0.69%) to 9,114.55, and the Shanghai Composite rose 72.62 points (+1.78%) to 4,163.10. Hong Kong's Hang Seng fell 156.29 points (-0.65%) to 23,768.52. AP's broader regional wrap also put Australia's S&P/ASX 200 down 0.1% to 8,816.10, Taiwan's Taiex up 2.8%, and India's Sensex up 0.5%.

value.vendor_basis_note: Trading Economics CFD-tracked JP225 item says +1,128 points / +1.58% to 72,379, while AP/MarketWatch cash close is 72,353.96; report uses AP/MarketWatch for close precision and Trading Economics only as directional corroboration

calculation.method: MarketWatch/AP cash close and AP rounded mover percentages; Trading Economics JP225 source is CFD-derived and therefore not used for cash close precision

text: Japan's Nikkei 225 closed at a fresh all-time high of 72,353.96 on Monday, June 22, up 1,103.90 points or 1.55%. AP attributed the record close to technology stocks fueled by enthusiasm over the global AI boom, with SoftBank up 1.9% and Tokyo Electron up 3.2%. Trading Economics independently showed the same direction but on a CFD-tracked JP225 basis (+1.58% to 72,379), so the report uses AP/MarketWatch for the cash-index close.

calculation.method: Yonhap close bulletin gives KOSPI 9,114.55 up 62.13 points; 62.13 divided by implied previous close 9,052.42 equals 0.69%; AP corroborates rounded +0.7% and record-close framing

text: South Korea's KOSPI closed at a record 9,114.55 on Monday, June 22, up 62.13 points or 0.69%. AP corroborated the record-close framing and said the gain was helped by AI-related shares, with memory chip maker SK Hynix up 5.6%.

value.holiday_basis: Both markets reopened after the Friday 2026-06-19 Dragon Boat/Tuen Ng holiday, so the comparison close is 2026-06-18

market_session: Hong Kong and Shanghai regular cash-equity closes, Monday 2026-06-22

calculation.method: Shanghai Composite: 4,163.10 close minus 4,090.48 prior listed close equals +72.62 points (+1.78%); Hang Seng: 23,768.52 close minus 23,924.81 prior listed close equals -156.29 points (-0.65%). HKEX calendar confirms June 19 Tuen Ng/Dragon Boat closure basis.

text: Greater China markets diverged after reopening from the June 19 Tuen Ng/Dragon Boat holiday. The Shanghai Composite closed at 4,163.10, up 72.62 points or 1.78% from the prior listed June 18 close, while the Hang Seng closed at 23,768.52, down 156.29 points or 0.65% from its prior listed June 18 close.

subject.name: US-Iran progress lowers oil stress while AI hardware leads North Asia

value.macro_support: Oil prices eased as US-Iran talks progressed, improving risk appetite
value.breadth_constraint: Elevated US-rate expectations limited broader enthusiasm
value.sector_leadership: AI and semiconductor-linked stocks led Japan and South Korea; Hong Kong and Australia lagged

market_session: Asia-Pacific regular sessions and early US premarket context, Monday 2026-06-22
text: The Asia session's macro support came from easing oil stress as US-Iran talks showed progress: AP reported Brent down about 2% to $79.02 and US crude down 74 cents to $75.11 early Monday. LGT also said most Asian equity markets rose as easing concern over US-Iran tensions improved risk appetite, though elevated US-rate expectations limited breadth. The leadership was narrower than the headline suggests: AI and semiconductor-linked shares drove Japan and Korea records, while Hong Kong and Australia lagged.
